matches.rationale: Same Edit trigger for files under repository subdirectories and the same requirement to read every README on the path.
Same Write trigger for creating files under repository subdirectories and the same README-chain requirement.
Same Edit target: Next.js source or integration tests, with the watch process required before changes. The extraction omits the already-running and explicit-skip-explanation clauses.
Same Bash target and substitution of the fast types command for a full Next.js build when checking only types.
Same branch-switch Bash trigger and full bootstrap build requirement.
Same Write target for new test-suite files and requirement to generate them with pnpm new-test. The extraction does not spell out the template structure/fixture rationale.
Same Bash target: avoid rerunning a suite for different grep filters and capture output once for later analysis.
Same Edit target for asynchronous waiting in tests, replacing setTimeout with retry().
Same Edit target banning deprecated check() and prescribing retry() plus expect().
Same Edit target for inline nextTestSetup files objects and preference for real fixture directories.
Same Bash target for local CI-failure reproduction with the exact CI environment, explicitly including IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1.
Same Bash target for creating a PR description and the required trailing NEXT_JS_LLM_PR comment. The regex covers creation rather than every possible update command.
Same Bash target for local test commands and injection of NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1.
Same Bash target forbidding secret values in commits or shared output. The extraction is narrowed to git commit and omits other emitting commands.
Same Bash target of constructing a local CI-equivalent command with exact environment flags, narrowed to snapshot-update test commands; it omits the no-literal-secret clause.
Same Bash target blocking staging local secret/environment files. The extraction omits the placeholder-only documentation clause.
Same Bash target blocking remote URL changes after SSH authentication failure unless requested.
Same Bash target for a force push or rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, requiring an SSH preflight.
Same Bash commit target and prohibition on generated-by or co-author footers.
Same Bash target requiring formatting/lint pre-validation before committing changed files.
Same Bash target blocking gh pr ready and leaving the PR in draft.
Same Bash target requiring a rebuild before Next.js integration tests after source changes.
Same Edit target for adding a feature flag and wiring its type, schema, and define-env entry where applicable.
Same Edit target for forcing Node-only-import feature flags false in edge builds in define-env.ts.
Same Edit target keeping react-server-dom-webpack imports in entry-base.ts and consuming exports elsewhere.
Same Edit target blocking relative internal requires from app-page.ts.
Same Edit target blocking browser.back() for the specified router-act accordion scenario.
Same Edit target avoiding new super:: imports in Rust, with the inline-mod exception.
Same Edit target for highlight attributes in documentation code blocks and accurate line counting. The extraction omits explicitly saying to highlight only relevant code.
Same Edit target for newly read nonstandard request headers in server code and security-review escalation.

misses.note: No Write rule starts or accounts for skipping the Next.js watch process when creating source or integration-test files; the extracted watch rule is Edit-only.
No extracted Bash rule preserves the already-started watch process throughout iterative development.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused test script by both development/production mode and Turbopack/Webpack bundler.
No extracted Bash rule handles end-of-session cleanup of a watch process started by the agent.
No extracted Bash rule prefers the filtered Next.js build specifically for an already-bootstrapped workspace with core-only edits.
No single extracted Bash rule covers full build-all for bootstrap, pre-CI, or cross-package changes; the checkout-only rule is reserved for the closer g11.
No extracted Bash rule forces Webpack with --webpack.
Compiler self-reported skip: "No existe la opción --no-turbopack: informational note about bundler flags, not an obligation tied to an action."
No Bash extraction binds pnpm new-test to test generation; the only new-test extraction is a Write rule used for g15.
No extracted Bash rule captures the non-interactive --args invocation and documented appDir/name/type argument format.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Ejecuta scripts/pr-status.js cuando el usuario pregunte por CI/PR: triggered by user intent, not by a tool-call precondition."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Reglas generales de triaje de PR/CI (prioriza bloqueantes, asume fallos reales, desconfía de flaky-list): general prioritization heuristics, not bound to one tool call."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Reglas generales de triaje de PR/CI (prioriza bloqueantes, asume fallos reales, desconfía de flaky-list): general prioritization heuristics, not bound to one tool call."
No extracted Bash rule requires the normal mode-specific test command for module-resolution or build-graph fixes.
No extracted Bash rule invokes the pr-status-triage skill for automated analysis.
No extracted Bash rule represents choosing CI failures ahead of review comments.
No extracted Bash rule maps rust check/build CI failures to cargo fmt -- --check followed by cargo fmt.
No extracted Bash rule maps lint/build Prettier failures to pnpm prettier --write on the affected file.
No extracted Bash rule requires running the specific CI-failing test path locally.
No extracted Bash rule chooses the test script matching the failing development or production mode.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Usa DEBUG=next:* para el registro de depuración: debugging tip, not tied to a specific tool call."
No extraction targets choosing NODE_ENV != production for development-bundle-only build-time guards; the DCE extraction concerns require placement, not this guard choice.
No extraction targets choosing __NEXT_DEV_SERVER for dev-server-only execution guards.
No extracted Bash rule treats all accessed environment-variable values as sensitive, apart from narrower commit-secret handling.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Si falta un secreto local obligatorio, detente y pregunta al usuario en vez de inventarlo: a judgment call about missing input, not tied to a specific tool call."
No extracted Bash rule requires concise, descriptive commit messages.
No extracted Bash rule requires PR descriptions to focus on what changed and why.

unmatched_extracted.note: Real document rule prohibiting generated-by/co-author footers in PRs; gold has the commit-targeted counterpart but no PR-footer rule.
Real action-bindable Edit rule requiring require() calls to stay behind compile-time branches for DCE; it is not semantically the NODE_ENV guard rule.
Real action-bindable Edit rule for wiring runtime environment values when a flag is used in precompiled runtime components; gold g50 covers the separate type/schema/user-bundle wiring.
Real Bash rule to cache an API response and avoid repeat queries for different analyses; no gold rule lists it.
Real Bash rule to group related edits and perform one build rather than one build per edit; no gold rule lists it.
